Haiden |名称=Ōhirasan Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirai-chō (Tochigi)|Q11482029|WD=Q11482029|lt=Hirai-chō|1=Hiraichō}} 659 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |創建={{ill|lt=827|Q30352|WD=Q30352|1=827}} ({{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 4) |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |例祭=October 18 |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Ōhirasan Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Ōhirasan Shrine''' (おおひらさんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a prefectural shrine. === Overview === The shrine stands at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}}, and the stone staircase on the front approach has about 1,000 steps. It is said to have been founded in 827 by Jikaku Daishi ({{ill|lt=Ennin|Q732282|WD=Q732282|1=Ennin}})[http://www.kuranomachi.jp/spot/ohirasan/jinja/ Tochigi City Tourism Association]. === Main enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. === Cultural properties === ;Tangible cultural properties designated by the city *Zuishin Gate (designated December 21, 1961) **Ceiling painting "Dragon" (designated December 21, 1961) *Main sanctuary (designated December 18, 2008) *Worship hall (designated December 18, 2008) *Hoshimiya Shrine building (designated December 18, 2008) === Other === The stone staircase on the approach is called Ajisai-zaka (Hydrangea Slope). Around 2,500 hydrangea plants of various kinds are planted thereYomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi edition, June 20, 2016, p.31.. === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Tochigi Station|Q7811990|WD=Q7811990|1=Tochigi Station}}, about 15 minutes by {{ill|lt=Kantō Jidōsha (Tochigi)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kantō Bus|1=Kanto Transportation}} on the Kokugakuin Line; get off at "Kokugakuin." *About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Tochigi Interchange|Q11110314|WD=Q11110314|lt=Tochigi IC|1=Tochigi interchange}} === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}} *{{ill|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park (Tochigi)|Q11443951|WD=Q11443951|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park|1=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park}} === External links === * {{Official website|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} - official website {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおひらさんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:栃木市の歴史]] [[Category:栃木市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

link|Q11443953}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |画像=[[File:Ohirasan shrine-haiden,tochigi-city,japan.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |名称=Ōhirasan Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirai-chō (Tochigi)|Q11482029|WD=Q11482029|lt=Hirai-chō|1=Hiraichō}} 659 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |創建={{ill|lt=827|Q30352|WD=Q30352|1=827}} ({{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 4) |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |例祭=October 18 |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Ōhirasan Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Ōhirasan Shrine''' (おおひらさんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a prefectural shrine. === Overview === The shrine stands at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}}, and the stone staircase on the front approach has about 1,000 steps. It is said to have been founded in 827 by Jikaku Daishi ({{ill|lt=Ennin|Q732282|WD=Q732282|1=Ennin}})[http://www.kuranomachi.jp/spot/ohirasan/jinja/ Tochigi City Tourism Association]. === Main enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. === Cultural properties === ;Tangible cultural properties designated by the city *Zuishin Gate (designated December 21, 1961) **Ceiling painting "Dragon" (designated December 21, 1961) *Main sanctuary (designated December 18, 2008) *Worship hall (designated December 18, 2008) *Hoshimiya Shrine building (designated December 18, 2008) === Other === The stone staircase on the approach is called Ajisai-zaka (Hydrangea Slope). Around 2,500 hydrangea plants of various kinds are planted thereYomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi edition, June 20, 2016, p.31.. === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Tochigi Station|Q7811990|WD=Q7811990|1=Tochigi Station}}, about 15 minutes by {{ill|lt=Kantō Jidōsha (Tochigi)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kantō Bus|1=Kanto Transportation}} on the Kokugakuin Line; get off at "Kokugakuin." *About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Tochigi Interchange|Q11110314|WD=Q11110314|lt=Tochigi IC|1=Tochigi interchange}} === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}} *{{ill|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park (Tochigi)|Q11443951|WD=Q11443951|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park|1=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park}} === External links === * {{Official website|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} - official website {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおひらさんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:栃木市の歴史]] [[Category:栃木市の建築物]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

link|Q11443953}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |画像=[[File:Ohirasan shrine-haiden,tochigi-city,japan.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |名称=Ōhirasan Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirai-chō (Tochigi)|Q11482029|WD=Q11482029|lt=Hirai-chō|1=Hiraichō}} 659 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |創建={{ill|lt=827|Q30352|WD=Q30352|1=827}} ({{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 4) |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |例祭=October 18 |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Ōhirasan Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Ōhirasan Shrine''' (おおひらさんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a prefectural shrine. === Overview === The shrine stands at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}}, and the stone staircase on the front approach has about 1,000 steps. It is said to have been founded in 827 by Jikaku Daishi ({{ill|lt=Ennin|Q732282|WD=Q732282|1=Ennin}})[http://www.kuranomachi.jp/spot/ohirasan/jinja/ Tochigi City Tourism Association]. === Main enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. === Cultural properties === ;Tangible cultural properties designated by the city *Zuishin Gate (designated December 21, 1961) **Ceiling painting "Dragon" (designated December 21, 1961) *Main sanctuary (designated December 18, 2008) *Worship hall (designated December 18, 2008) *Hoshimiya Shrine building (designated December 18, 2008) === Other === The stone staircase on the approach is called Ajisai-zaka (Hydrangea Slope). Around 2,500 hydrangea plants of various kinds are planted thereYomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi edition, June 20, 2016, p.31.. === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Tochigi Station|Q7811990|WD=Q7811990|1=Tochigi Station}}, about 15 minutes by {{ill|lt=Kantō Jidōsha (Tochigi)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kantō Bus|1=Kanto Transportation}} on the Kokugakuin Line; get off at "Kokugakuin." *About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Tochigi Interchange|Q11110314|WD=Q11110314|lt=Tochigi IC|1=Tochigi interchange}} === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}} *{{ill|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park (Tochigi)|Q11443951|WD=Q11443951|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park|1=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park}} === External links === * {{Official website|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} - official website {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:栃木市の歴史]] [[Category:栃木市の建築物]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

link|Q11443953}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |画像=[[File:Ohirasan shrine-haiden,tochigi-city,japan.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |名称=Ōhirasan Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirai-chō (Tochigi)|Q11482029|WD=Q11482029|lt=Hirai-chō|1=Hiraichō}} 659 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |創建={{ill|lt=827|Q30352|WD=Q30352|1=827}} ({{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 4) |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |例祭=October 18 |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Ōhirasan Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Ōhirasan Shrine''' (おおひらさんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a prefectural shrine. === Overview === The shrine stands at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}}, and the stone staircase on the front approach has about 1,000 steps. It is said to have been founded in 827 by Jikaku Daishi ({{ill|lt=Ennin|Q732282|WD=Q732282|1=Ennin}})[http://www.kuranomachi.jp/spot/ohirasan/jinja/ Tochigi City Tourism Association]. === Main enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. === Cultural properties === ;Tangible cultural properties designated by the city *Zuishin Gate (designated December 21, 1961) **Ceiling painting "Dragon" (designated December 21, 1961) *Main sanctuary (designated December 18, 2008) *Worship hall (designated December 18, 2008) *Hoshimiya Shrine building (designated December 18, 2008) === Other === The stone staircase on the approach is called Ajisai-zaka (Hydrangea Slope). Around 2,500 hydrangea plants of various kinds are planted thereYomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi edition, June 20, 2016, p.31.. === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Tochigi Station|Q7811990|WD=Q7811990|1=Tochigi Station}}, about 15 minutes by {{ill|lt=Kantō Jidōsha (Tochigi)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kantō Bus|1=Kanto Transportation}} on the Kokugakuin Line; get off at "Kokugakuin." *About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Tochigi Interchange|Q11110314|WD=Q11110314|lt=Tochigi IC|1=Tochigi interchange}} === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}} *{{ill|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park (Tochigi)|Q11443951|WD=Q11443951|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park|1=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park}} === External links === * {{Official website|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} - official website {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:栃木市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Tochigi, Tochigi]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden |名称=Ōhirasan Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirai-chō (Tochigi)|Q11482029|WD=Q11482029|lt=Hirai-chō|1=Hiraichō}} 659 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |創建={{ill|lt=827|Q30352|WD=Q30352|1=827}} ({{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 4) |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |例祭=October 18 |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Ōhirasan Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Ōhirasan Shrine''' (おおひらさんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a prefectural shrine. === Overview === The shrine stands at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}}, and the stone staircase on the front approach has about 1,000 steps. It is said to have been founded in 827 by Jikaku Daishi ({{ill|lt=Ennin|Q732282|WD=Q732282|1=Ennin}})[http://www.kuranomachi.jp/spot/ohirasan/jinja/ Tochigi City Tourism Association]. === Main enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. === Cultural properties === ;Tangible cultural properties designated by the city *Zuishin Gate (designated December 21, 1961) **Ceiling painting "Dragon" (designated December 21, 1961) *Main sanctuary (designated December 18, 2008) *Worship hall (designated December 18, 2008) *Hoshimiya Shrine building (designated December 18, 2008) === Other === The stone staircase on the approach is called Ajisai-zaka (Hydrangea Slope). Around 2,500 hydrangea plants of various kinds are planted thereYomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi edition, June 20, 2016, p.31.. === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Tochigi Station|Q7811990|WD=Q7811990|1=Tochigi Station}}, about 15 minutes by {{ill|lt=Kantō Jidōsha (Tochigi)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kantō Bus|1=Kanto Transportation}} on the Kokugakuin Line; get off at "Kokugakuin." *About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Tochigi Interchange|Q11110314|WD=Q11110314|lt=Tochigi IC|1=Tochigi interchange}} === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}} *{{ill|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park (Tochigi)|Q11443951|WD=Q11443951|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park|1=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park}} === External links === * {{Official website|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} - official website {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:栃木市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Tochigi, Tochigi]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

link|Q11443953}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |画像=[[File:Ohirasan shrine-haiden,tochigi-city,japan.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |名称=Ōhirasan Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirai-chō (Tochigi)|Q11482029|WD=Q11482029|lt=Hirai-chō|1=Hiraichō}} 659 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |創建={{ill|lt=827|Q30352|WD=Q30352|1=827}} ({{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 4) |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |例祭=October 18 |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Ōhirasan Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Ōhirasan Shrine''' (おおひらさんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a prefectural shrine. === Overview === The shrine stands at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}}, and the stone staircase on the front approach has about 1,000 steps. It is said to have been founded in 827 by Jikaku Daishi ({{ill|lt=Ennin|Q732282|WD=Q732282|1=Ennin}})[http://www.kuranomachi.jp/spot/ohirasan/jinja/ Tochigi City Tourism Association]. === Main enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. === Cultural properties === ;Tangible cultural properties designated by the city *Zuishin Gate (designated December 21, 1961) **Ceiling painting "Dragon" (designated December 21, 1961) *Main sanctuary (designated December 18, 2008) *Worship hall (designated December 18, 2008) *Hoshimiya Shrine building (designated December 18, 2008) === Other === The stone staircase on the approach is called Ajisai-zaka (Hydrangea Slope). Around 2,500 hydrangea plants of various kinds are planted thereYomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi edition, June 20, 2016, p.31.. === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Tochigi Station|Q7811990|WD=Q7811990|1=Tochigi Station}}, about 15 minutes by {{ill|lt=Kantō Jidōsha (Tochigi)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kantō Bus|1=Kanto Transportation}} on the Kokugakuin Line; get off at "Kokugakuin." *About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Tochigi Interchange|Q11110314|WD=Q11110314|lt=Tochigi IC|1=Tochigi interchange}} === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}} *{{ill|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park (Tochigi)|Q11443951|WD=Q11443951|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park|1=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park}} === External links === * {{Official website|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} - official website {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:栃木市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Tochigi, Tochigi]]

link|Q11443953}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |画像=[[File:Ohirasan shrine-haiden,tochigi-city,japan.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |名称=Ōhirasan Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirai-chō (Tochigi)|Q11482029|WD=Q11482029|lt=Hirai-chō|1=Hiraichō}} 659 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |創建={{ill|lt=827|Q30352|WD=Q30352|1=827}} ({{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 4) |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |例祭=October 18 |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Ōhirasan Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Ōhirasan Shrine''' (おおひらさんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a prefectural shrine. === Overview === The shrine stands at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}}, and the stone staircase on the front approach has about 1,000 steps. It is said to have been founded in 827 by Jikaku Daishi ({{ill|lt=Ennin|Q732282|WD=Q732282|1=Ennin}})[http://www.kuranomachi.jp/spot/ohirasan/jinja/ Tochigi City Tourism Association]. === Main enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. === Cultural properties === ;Tangible cultural properties designated by the city *Zuishin Gate (designated December 21, 1961) **Ceiling painting "Dragon" (designated December 21, 1961) *Main sanctuary (designated December 18, 2008) *Worship hall (designated December 18, 2008) *Hoshimiya Shrine building (designated December 18, 2008) === Other === The stone staircase on the approach is called Ajisai-zaka (Hydrangea Slope). Around 2,500 hydrangea plants of various kinds are planted thereYomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi edition, June 20, 2016, p.31.. === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Tochigi Station|Q7811990|WD=Q7811990|1=Tochigi Station}}, about 15 minutes by {{ill|lt=Kantō Jidōsha (Tochigi)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kantō Bus|1=Kanto Transportation}} on the Kokugakuin Line; get off at "Kokugakuin." *About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Tochigi Interchange|Q11110314|WD=Q11110314|lt=Tochigi IC|1=Tochigi interchange}} === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}} *{{ill|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park (Tochigi)|Q11443951|WD=Q11443951|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park|1=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park}} === External links === * {{Official website|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} - official website {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:栃木市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Tochigi, Tochigi]]

Haiden |名称=Ōhirasan Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirai-chō (Tochigi)|Q11482029|WD=Q11482029|lt=Hirai-chō|1=Hiraichō}} 659 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |創建={{ill|lt=827|Q30352|WD=Q30352|1=827}} ({{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 4) |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |例祭=October 18 |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Ōhirasan Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|45.2|N|139|41|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Ōhirasan Shrine''' (おおひらさんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tochigi|Q44830|WD=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a prefectural shrine. === Overview === The shrine stands at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}}, and the stone staircase on the front approach has about 1,000 steps. It is said to have been founded in 827 by Jikaku Daishi ({{ill|lt=Ennin|Q732282|WD=Q732282|1=Ennin}})[http://www.kuranomachi.jp/spot/ohirasan/jinja/ Tochigi City Tourism Association]. === Main enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. === Cultural properties === ;Tangible cultural properties designated by the city *Zuishin Gate (designated December 21, 1961) **Ceiling painting "Dragon" (designated December 21, 1961) *Main sanctuary (designated December 18, 2008) *Worship hall (designated December 18, 2008) *Hoshimiya Shrine building (designated December 18, 2008) === Other === The stone staircase on the approach is called Ajisai-zaka (Hydrangea Slope). Around 2,500 hydrangea plants of various kinds are planted thereYomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi edition, June 20, 2016, p.31.. === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Tochigi Station|Q7811990|WD=Q7811990|1=Tochigi Station}}, about 15 minutes by {{ill|lt=Kantō Jidōsha (Tochigi)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kantō Bus|1=Kanto Transportation}} on the Kokugakuin Line; get off at "Kokugakuin." *About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Tochigi Interchange|Q11110314|WD=Q11110314|lt=Tochigi IC|1=Tochigi interchange}} === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Mount Ōhira (Tochigi)|Q11443944|WD=Q11443944|lt=Mount Ōhira|1=Mount Ōhira}} *{{ill|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park (Tochigi)|Q11443951|WD=Q11443951|lt=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park|1=Ōhirasan Prefectural Natural Park}} === External links === * {{Official website|name=Ōhirasan Shrine}} - official website {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōhirasan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:栃木市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tochigi, Tochigi]]
